metadata.source: Official compliance framework publications - PCI-DSS v4.0.1, HIPAA, SOX, NIST CSF 2.0

pci_dss.applicable_to: Any organization that accepts, processes, stores, or transmits cardholder data

requirement_1.title: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls

1.1.title: Processes and mechanisms for network security controls are defined and understood
requirements: All security controls documented with justification
Configuration standards for NSCs created and kept current
Roles and responsibilities assigned

1.2.title: Network security controls (NSCs) are configured and maintained
requirements: Changes to network connections reviewed and approved
Diagram of all connections between CDE and other networks maintained
All connections between CDE and other networks reviewed at least once every six months
technical_controls: Inbound and outbound traffic restricted to what is necessary
All other traffic denied by default
NSC configurations reviewed at least every six months

1.3.title: Network access to and from the cardholder data environment is restricted
requirements: Inbound traffic to CDE from untrusted networks restricted to only that necessary
Outbound traffic from CDE restricted to only that necessary
NSC connections between CDE and other networks prohibited except as necessary
All other traffic denied by default (default deny)

specific_controls.direct_internet_access: Prohibited - no direct public access between internet and CDE (Req 1.3.2)
specific_controls.dmz_requirement: All internet-facing services placed in DMZ separate from CDE
specific_controls.firewall_rules: Document all deny rules, prohibit any-any rules
specific_controls.stateful_inspection: NSCs must be stateful - track connection state

1.4.title: Network connections between trusted and untrusted networks are controlled
requirements: NSCs implemented between all wireless networks and CDE
NSCs implemented between all untrusted networks and CDE
Traffic from wireless classified as untrusted unless appropriate controls in place

1.5.title: Risks to CDE from computing devices able to connect to both untrusted networks and CDE are mitigated
requirements: Security policy implemented for devices that can connect to both internet and CDE
Personal firewall or equivalent security software deployed
Security software not alterable by device users

requirement_2.title: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components

2.2.title: System components are configured and managed securely
requirements: Configuration standards developed for all system component types
All default passwords changed before installation
Unnecessary functions, ports, protocols removed
One primary function per server (web server, DB server not combined unless virtualized)

in_transit.requirement: Strong cryptography for all data transmission over open public networks (Req 4.2.1)
in_transit.cipher_suites: Only strong cipher suites - AESGCM, ChaCha20-Poly1305

options: One-way hash of entire PAN (SHA-256 minimum)
Index tokens with secure pad in secure keystore

requirement_10.title: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
log_what: All individual user access to cardholder data (Req 10.2.1.1)
All actions taken by root or administrative privileges (Req 10.2.1.2)
Use of and changes to identification and authentication mechanisms
Initialization, stopping, or pausing of audit logs (Req 10.2.1.6)
Creation and deletion of system-level objects (Req 10.2.1.7)

retention.note: Logs must be protected from modification

requirement_10.time_sync: All system clocks synchronized using NTP to trusted time sources

requirement_11.quarterly_scans: External vulnerability scans by PCI-approved scanning vendor (ASV) quarterly
requirement_11.internal_scans: Internal vulnerability scans at least quarterly and after significant changes

penetration_testing.after_changes: After significant infrastructure or application upgrade
penetration_testing.scope: External and internal penetration testing per PCI-defined methodology

cde_segmentation.description: Cardholder Data Environment must be isolated from other networks
requirements: Documented network diagrams showing CDE scope
All connections to CDE controlled by NSC (firewall, router ACL)
Wireless networks treated as untrusted unless assessed and isolated
Segmentation verified at least every six months by qualified internal resource or ASV

vlan_requirements.cde_vlan: Dedicated VLAN for cardholder data systems
vlan_requirements.access_control: ACL or firewall between CDE VLAN and all other VLANs
vlan_requirements.monitoring: All traffic to/from CDE logged and monitored

hipaa.full_name: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
hipaa.effective: 1996 (Privacy Rule 2003, Security Rule 2005, Breach Notification 2009, Omnibus 2013)
hipaa.applicable_to: Covered entities (healthcare providers, insurers, clearinghouses) and business associates

administrative_safeguards.security_officer: Designate security official responsible for policies and procedures
administrative_safeguards.workforce_training: Training program for all workforce members

access_management.authorization: Policies for granting access to PHI based on role

administrative_safeguards.incident_procedures: Document and respond to suspected security incidents

contingency_plan.disaster_recovery: Restore loss of data in emergency
contingency_plan.emergency_mode_operations: Procedures to protect security of ePHI during emergency

physical_safeguards.facility_access: Procedures to limit physical access to ePHI
physical_safeguards.workstation_use: Policies on workstation functions and physical attributes
physical_safeguards.data_disposal: Final disposition of ePHI and hardware/media
physical_safeguards.media_reuse: Procedures to remove ePHI before reuse

access_control.unique_user_ids: Assign unique name/number for identifying/tracking user identity
access_control.emergency_access: Procedures to obtain ePHI during emergency
access_control.automatic_logoff: Terminate electronic sessions after predetermined period of inactivity
access_control.encryption_decryption: Mechanism to encrypt and decrypt ePHI (addressable)

audit_controls.description: Hardware, software, or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in systems containing ePHI
audit_controls.requirements: Log access to ePHI - who accessed, when, what records

integrity.mechanism: Electronic mechanism to corroborate ePHI not altered or destroyed
integrity.transmission_security: Guard against unauthorized access during transmission

transmission_security.encryption: Encryption of ePHI in transit - recommended (addressable)
transmission_security.vpn: Encrypted VPN tunnels for ePHI across untrusted networks

segmentation.phi_systems: Isolate systems containing PHI from general network
segmentation.visitor_network: Completely separate network for patient/visitor WiFi

encryption_standards.email: S/MIME or PGP for ePHI in email
encryption_standards.mobile_devices: Full-device encryption for mobile devices with ePHI

access_controls.authentication: Multi-factor authentication recommended for ePHI access
access_controls.minimum_necessary: Users only access PHI required for their job function
access_controls.audit_logging: Log all access to PHI with user ID, date/time, action taken
access_controls.remote_access: VPN with MFA for remote access to systems with PHI

timeline.hhs_notification: 60 days from end of calendar year (if < 500 individuals), 60 days from discovery (if >= 500)
timeline.media_notice: Breaches >= 500 in a state - prominent media outlets in that state

sox.applicable_to: Publicly traded companies in the United States

section_302.title: Corporate responsibility for financial reports
section_302.it_requirement: CEOs and CFOs certify accuracy of financial statements - requires trusted IT systems

section_404.title: Management assessment of internal controls
section_404.requirement: Annual assessment of effectiveness of internal control over financial reporting

access_controls.logical_access: Only authorized users access financial systems
access_controls.privileged_access: Segregation of duties - developers cannot access production
access_controls.user_provisioning: Formal process for granting, modifying, removing access
access_controls.periodic_reviews: Quarterly access reviews of all financial system users

change_management.change_control: All changes to financial systems go through formal change management
change_management.testing: All changes tested in non-production before deployment
change_management.emergency_changes: Emergency change process with post-implementation review

it_operations.backup_recovery: Financial data backed up and recovery tested
it_operations.job_scheduling: Critical batch jobs monitored for success/failure
it_operations.incident_management: Incidents affecting financial systems tracked and resolved

systems_development.sdlc: Formal SDLC methodology with security requirements
systems_development.testing: Unit, integration, and user acceptance testing

requirements: Log all access to financial systems
Log all changes to financial data
Log all privileged actions
Logs immutable and tamper-evident
Retention minimum 7 years (documents) / 5 years (audit work papers)

siem_requirements.collection: Central log collection from all financial systems

network_requirements.segmentation: Financial systems isolated from non-financial systems
network_requirements.access_control: Network ACLs restricting access to financial systems by source IP
network_requirements.monitoring: Network traffic to financial systems monitored and logged
network_requirements.remote_access: All remote access to financial systems via MFA VPN

govern.description: NEW in CSF 2.0 - Organizational context, risk management strategy, cybersecurity supply chain risk management
outcomes: GV.SC: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management

identify.description: Asset management, business environment, governance, risk assessment, risk management strategy
outcomes: ID.AM: Asset Management - inventory all hardware, software, data
ID.BE: Business Environment - critical functions and dependencies
ID.RA: Risk Assessment - vulnerability assessment and threat analysis
ID.RM: Risk Management Strategy - risk tolerance and priorities

protect.description: Identity management, awareness training, data security, information protection, maintenance
outcomes: PR.AA: Identity Management and Access Control

detect.description: Anomalies and events, security monitoring, detection processes

respond.description: Response planning, communications, analysis, mitigation, improvements

controls: Encryption at rest and in transit

iso_27001.applicable_to: Any organization seeking to manage information security risks

A.8.20.title: Networks security
requirements: Networks managed to protect information in systems and applications
Controls implemented to protect information in networks

A.8.21.title: Security of network services
requirements: Security mechanisms, service levels, and management requirements identified
Security features of network services included in network service agreements

A.8.22.title: Segregation of networks
requirements: Groups of information services, users, and information systems segregated
Network perimeters defined and access between perimeters controlled

fedramp.description: Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program
fedramp.applicable_to: Cloud service providers serving US federal agencies

impact_levels.high: Severe or catastrophic adverse effect (law enforcement, healthcare)

network_requirements.boundary_protection: Managed interfaces at organizational system boundaries
network_requirements.network_segmentation: Internal network separated from external networks
